user_query: Update mothers teriyaki salmon bowl lunch to a tofu based dish

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

short_description.from: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
short_description.to: Pan-seared marinated firm tofu glazed with a homemade teriyaki sauce, served over steamed rice with steamed broccoli, pickled ginger, and a sprinkling of sesame and nori — a satisfying plant-based take on a classic donburi.

nutrition_note.from: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.
nutrition_note.to: Tofu provides high-quality plant protein and is lower in saturated fat than salmon; it also supplies isoflavones and, if fortified, calcium and iron. Swapping salmon for tofu reduces omega-3 content, so include other omega-3 sources during the day if needed (e.g., flaxseeds, walnuts). Making teriyaki at home with low-sodium soy sauce and reduced sweetener keeps sodium and added sugars in check. Pairing tofu with broccoli and rice preserves a balanced plate of protein, fiber, vitamins, and carbohydrates.

meal_suggestion.from: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
meal_suggestion.to: Press firm tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut into slabs or cubes. Marinate briefly in a mix of low-sodium soy sauce, mirin, sake (or rice vinegar), grated ginger, and a touch of sugar or maple syrup. Pan-sear or bake the tofu until golden and firm, then reduce the marinade into a glossy teriyaki glaze and toss the tofu to coat. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli on the side, a small mound of pickled ginger, and garnish with toasted sesame seeds, sliced nori, and thinly sliced scallions. For a lighter version, use less sugar and low-sodium soy sauce, and consider using brown rice for added fiber.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the teriyaki salmon donburi into a tofu-based teriyaki donburi. Rewrote name, description, meal suggestion, ingredients, and nutrition note to reflect the plant-based substitution and updated preparation details.
